content:
2 min read
23 hours ago

--

This one will be a short today!
I’ve spent a good deal of time writing Angular code in my career and one thing that has stood out to me is how difficult can be to integrate other web technologies into the Angular ecosystem. There are plenty of technologies out there that try to make CSS frameworks and JavaScript libraries play nice with Angular by creating a bridge between the rest of the web world and Angular (I like to think of them as “wrapper tech”).
A good example of this is a project called ng-bootstrap (https://ng-bootstrap.github.io). This Angular technology is designed to bridge the gap between Bootstrap (https://getbootstrap.com/), a component and CSS framework for native JS and HTML applications, and Angular projects. I think it's a good technology and I've used it on a number of occasions. However, nothing is perfect and there are trade offs with everything.
There are plenty of times when these “wrapper” technologies will work against you and knowing how to roll your own solution is the best path forward. If you wish to use Bootstrap in your project and don’t want to use a technology like ng-bootstrap, there is an easy way to add Bootstrap and start writing your own components using the technology directly.
How to Add Bootstrap
Step 1
Add the Bootstrap dependency to your project with this command.
npm install bootstrapStep 2
Next, just add the JavaScript and CSS files to your angular.json file like this:
{
...
"styles": [
"node_modules/bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css",
"src/styles.css"
],
"scripts": ["node_modules/bootstrap/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.js"]
...
}Note: Both the styles and scripts properties should be placed in the options block of the config, here is the path where it can be found: /projects/psych/architect/build/options
That’s it! Once you have this in place you can follow the documentation on the Bootstrap website to add the proper styles and functionality to your components.
